_comment: State-level election compass template for India Election Compass. Copy this file to elections/{state}/{year-level}/config.json and fill in actual content. Remove all _comment fields before deploying.
_algorithm_note: Use 'hybrid' (5 user answer buttons) for richer discrimination. Alternatives: 'cityblock/approve-neutral-reject' (3 buttons). Party positions always use approve/neutral/reject/skip regardless of algorithm.
_languages_note: Add one object per UI language. 'code' must be a BCP-47 code (e.g. 'ta', 'kn', 'ml', 'te', 'hi'). All multilingual content fields below must have a key for EVERY code listed here.

subtitle.en: Find which party best matches your views on key {State} policy issues

text.en: Describe the election, the parties covered, how many theses, and that the tool is non-partisan.

parties._comment: Repeat this block for every party. 'alias' is used as the key in thesis positions.

description.en: 2-3 sentence factual description: founding, ideology, current leadership, key stands.

theses._comment: Repeat for each policy question. Use 10-20 theses for best user experience.

statement.en: State the policy position as a clear, specific, testable statement. Avoid vague language. Good: 'The government should abolish X.' Bad: 'The government should think about X.'

party1._position_values: approve | neutral | reject | skip (skip hides the party from this thesis)

explanation.en: 1-2 sentences citing concrete evidence: manifesto pledge, legislation, voting record, public statement.

_deployment_note: Place this file at: elections/{state-code}/{year-level}/config.json. Link to it from compass.html?config=elections/{state-code}/{year-level}/config.json and from index.html.
